title: GSM8K & HumanEval: Math & Code
concept: GSM8K evaluates multi-step grade-school math reasoning; HumanEval tests coding capability by checking if generated python programs pass unit tests.

exercises.prompt: Why do researchers test AI models using benchmarks like GSM8K and HumanEval?

options.text: To test and compare their reasoning and coding skills
To teach them how to chat with humans
To make them run faster on computers

exercises.explain: Standardized benchmarks like GSM8K and HumanEval help researchers measure and compare how well different AI models can reason and code.

cards.title: AI High School
cards.body: How do we know if an AI is actually smart, or if it is just repeating words it memorized? We put it through school! Specifically, we test its ability to solve math word problems and write computer code.

cards.title: GSM8K & HumanEval
cards.body: GSM8K and HumanEval are standardized tests for AIs. GSM8K uses thousands of high-quality, multi-step math word problems to test logic. HumanEval uses Python coding tasks that are verified by running real tests to make sure the code actually works.

cards.title: The Kitchen Test
cards.body: Imagine teaching a robot to cook. GSM8K is like giving it a written recipe with several steps to see if it can figure out the measurements. HumanEval is like putting the finished dish in front of a food critic who tastes it (running unit tests) to see if it was made correctly.

cards.title: Real-world Benchmarks
cards.body: A GSM8K task might ask: 'If Maya has 5 apples and eats 2, then buys double her remaining amount, how many does she have?' (Answer: 9). A HumanEval task might ask the AI to write a Python function `reverse_string(s)` and then run tests like checking if `reverse_string('cat')` equals `'tac'`.

cards.title: Looks Good vs. Actually Works
cards.body: Many people believe that if an AI can write code that looks correct, it must be right. But code can have tiny, hidden bugs! That is why HumanEval does not just read the code—it actually runs it through strict unit tests to make sure it functions perfectly.

cards.title: Math vs. Code
cards.body: Let's compare the focus and validation methods of these two benchmarks.

left.text: Tests multi-step math reasoning using word problems with final numeric answers.

right.text: Tests coding ability by running python scripts against automated unit tests.

exercises.prompt: What kind of problems does the GSM8K benchmark use to test an AI?

options.text: Grade-school math word problems requiring multiple steps

exercises.explain: GSM8K consists of grade-school level math word problems that require multiple steps of logical reasoning to solve.

exercises.prompt: True or False: GSM8K is the primary benchmark used to test Python coding capability.
exercises.explain: GSM8K focuses on multi-step math reasoning, while HumanEval is the benchmark specifically designed to test Python coding capabilities.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the explanation of how HumanEval checks coding ability.
exercises.sentence: HumanEval evaluates code by running it against several ______ to check if it works.
exercises.explain: HumanEval requires the AI's generated Python code to pass unit tests to verify that it actually runs and solves the problem correctly.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following statements about coding benchmarks is correct?

options.text: HumanEval checks coding correctness using unit tests.
GSM8K evaluates how fast an AI can write Python code.
HumanEval only grades code based on how neat it looks.

exercises.explain: HumanEval tests Python coding capability, while GSM8K focuses on math word problems. The third option is incorrect because AI code must pass tests to be considered successful on HumanEval.

exercises.prompt: You are building a new AI and want to prove it can both solve complex math word problems and write working code. Which benchmarks should you choose?

options.text: GSM8K tests grade-school math word problems; HumanEval tests Python coding with unit tests.
GSM8K tests python programming; HumanEval tests simple history trivia.
GSM8K tests how fast an AI runs; HumanEval tests how creative its stories are.
Both benchmarks only test how many words an AI can memorize from the internet.

exercises.explain: GSM8K acts as a test for multi-step math reasoning (using word problems), while HumanEval acts as a test for python coding ability (using actual unit tests to verify if the code works).
